The bioactive compounds found in Capsicum ligustrinum include capsaicinoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. Capsicum ligustrinum is often used in herbal preparations such as capsules, tinctures, and topical creams to harness its potential therapeutic benefits.
